summary: End-to-end frontend testing of new portfolio features (case study deep-dive modal, About headshot + Download CV, Read CV in contact). 15/16 checks pass. The only failure is the About headshot: PROFILE.photo is still null in /app/frontend/src/data/content.js, so the never renders — the monogram fallback shows instead. The /linko.jpg asset itself is correctly served (HTTP 200, image/jpeg, 37,649 bytes) and exists in /app/frontend/public/linko.jpg; only the wiring in content.js is missing. Everything else in the review request works as specified.

ui_bugs.issue: PROFILE.photo is null in /app/frontend/src/data/content.js, so AboutSection renders the monogram 'LJ' placeholder instead of the . The asset exists at /app/frontend/public/linko.jpg and is served with HTTP 200 + image/jpeg, but it is never referenced because content.js still has `photo: null`. One-line fix: change `photo: null` -> `photo: '/linko.jpg'`.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/data/content.js set `PROFILE.photo = '/linko.jpg'` (currently null) so the About section renders the data-testid='about-photo' image instead of the monogram fallback. The file is already in /app/frontend/public/linko.jpg and serves 200 image/jpeg.

(Optional, low priority) The review request listed `nav-link-work` as a bottom-bar testid, but the actual generated testid is `nav-link-case-1` (label text is still 'Work'). The agent-to-agent context note in the request already acknowledged this. If you want the testid to literally be `nav-link-work`, you'd need to add a label-derived alias in /app/frontend/src/components/Nav.jsx; otherwise tests should target `nav-link-case-1`.
critical_code_review_comments: content.js: PROFILE.photo is still hard-coded to null even though /linko.jpg is now shipped in public/. This is the single-line miss behind the about-photo regression.

Nav.jsx NAV_LINKS uses section ids for testids (`nav-link-${l.id}`), which gives `nav-link-case-1` for the 'Work' link. Slightly counter-intuitive — consider deriving the testid from the label slug (`nav-link-work`) for clearer test contracts.

CaseModal closes on Escape via a window keydown listener that is attached on mount and never gated on `data`. It is benign (caseModal.close() is a no-op when nothing is open), but adding an `if (!data) return` guard inside the handler would prevent unrelated Escape presses from invoking the store.

caseModal.open/close mutates document.body.style.overflow directly. If a user navigates away while the modal is open (e.g. via deep-link routing later), the scroll lock would leak. Currently fine because the app has no router.
seed_data_creation: Added one TEST_E2E contact row via POST /api/contact during the contact-form flow check. Safe to ignore — flagged with TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent flips PROFILE.photo to '/linko.jpg', re-verify just the About section: scroll to section-about (~1/8 of maxScroll) and confirm `document.querySelector('[data-testid=about-photo]').naturalWidth > 0` and src ends with '/linko.jpg'. All other flows already pass: deep-dive modal (case-open-01/02/03) opens with overview + 'The work' (3 step images all loaded) + 'Highlights' (3 chips) + 'Outcome' + tags, closes via case-modal-close button, Escape key, and dark-backdrop click; body.style.overflow is locked to 'hidden' while open and restored to '' after close. Nav prev-arrow is disabled at intro, next-arrow advances, bottom-bar links (nav-link-intro/about/experience/case-1/process/skills/contact) jump correctly, keyboard ArrowDown/ArrowUp/Home/End all change scrollY. Tunnel <canvas> pixel fingerprints at 0%/50%/95% differ (canvas scrubs). Section panels apply translateX (xPercent) during scroll — horizontal motion confirmed via computed transform matrices. Contact form POST /api/contact -> 200 with 'Message sent' confirmation, and a 'Read CV' link is present in the contact links list. The /linko.jpg asset returns 200 image/jpeg.
rca of the issue:
About headshot regression: the new headshot file was added to /app/frontend/public/linko.jpg (and is served correctly by the dev server with HTTP 200, content-type image/jpeg, 37,649 bytes), but /app/frontend/src/data/content.js still has `photo: null` at PROFILE.photo. AboutSection in /app/frontend/src/components/Sections.jsx renders the only when PROFILE.photo is truthy; otherwise it falls back to a monogram
LJ
. So the data-testid='about-photo' element never enters the DOM. Reproduction: open /?nointro, scroll to ~1/8 of maxScroll (section-about), query [data-testid='about-photo'] — returns null. Mitigation: set PROFILE.photo = '/linko.jpg' in content.js (one-line change).
